content.answer:
Hi Ian,

Please find attached monthly SIP update for our discussion on coming monday.

Thank you.
Cuiwei

content.question:
Thanks.



Slide 5

   - Seller PC2 here includes fee that we pay to platform?

Do we get any
   discount to the fee?
- Why is CN SIP so negative on PC2?
What was it previously?
- Can we also show the MP PC2 and SLS P&L as separate row items?

Slide 6

   - Local SIP PC2 is negative at negative?
- Can we also show the MP PC2 and SLS P&L as separate row items?
- CN SIP is growing very slowly MOM?

At such a low base, I would have
   thought that it should be growing much faster?
- WH Cross selling does not feel like it is growing much as well?

Have
   we open it up to Local/CB FBS yet?

Slide 7

   - Any sense why IDMY is 1 more day longer than IDSG?

Slide 8

   - Where did we land on the fraud situation?

Slide 9

   - Do not quite understand what the issue is here. Think the our main
   contribution for these markets will come from CNSIP, so we can rely on CN
   SLS side?

Slide 12

   - Growth feels very low for the # of new SKUs that we brought live?

Do
   we know why?
Are the numbers within expectation?

Slide 14

   - For new sellers, why % of SKUs dropping?
- For rejected sellers, acceptance rate is just too low?

And why % of
   SKU acceptance also so low?
- What are the reasons for rejection right now?

We have to address it 1
   by 1?

Slide 15

   - #1/#3/#4 are probably all not valid?

How exactly are we addressing
   this?

Slide 17

   - How do we decide what SKUs to incubate with ads?

And how do we decide
   how many SKUs to incubate?
- How do we get these SKUs into CSPU pool (if applicable)?

Where they
   will benefit from organic traffic is the prices are good?

Slide 19

   - Does it make sense to onboard so many SKUs in one go?

Or does it make
   sense to stagger and incubate them properly?

Slide 20

   - What does this extra budget that we are referring to?

There is no
   extra budget that we can ask from local markets, I am not sure why we keep
   bringing this concept up



Slide 25

   - What is this full BPSF fee waiver about?

Slide 26

   - What is this PRM support about?

Slide 30

   - Average waiting time is so long?
When are we solving this?
- Where do I see the Shopbot CSAT?
- When are we taking over the management of CS for each import market?

In general, performance does not seem to see any improvement











*From:* Ian Ho <hoi@sea.com>
*Sent:* Monday, 21 July 2025 12:33 pm
*To:* 'Cuiwei Sun' <cuiwei.sun@shopee.com>
*Cc:* 'Jianghong Liu' <jianghong.liu@shopee.com>; 'Shuning Wang' <
shuning.wang@shopee.com>; 'Keljin Chua' <keljin.chua@shopee.com>; 'Jason
Yu' <jason.yu@shopee.com>; 'Yu Zhang' <yu.zhang@shopee.com>; 'Ailin Xie' <
ailin.xie@shopee.com>; 'Wei Qiang' <wei.qiang@shopee.com>; 'Chiong Zhi Ann'
<zhiann.chiong@shopee.com>; 'Huang Wan-Jung' <wanjung.huangwj@shopee.com>;
'Nicholas Lim' <nicholas.lim@shopee.com>; 'Keith Huang' <
keith.huang@shopee.com>; 'Joe Li' <joe.liyc@shopee.com>; 'Lim Wan Ting' <
wanting.lim@shopee.com>; 'Ren Jiahui' <jiahui.ren@shopee.com>; 'Eveline
Seah' <eveline.seahyl@shopee.com>; 'Lee Jia Yi' <jiayi.jylee@shopee.com>;
'Fandi Nie' <fandi.nie@shopee.com>; 'Faye Yan' <faye.yan@shopee.com>
*Subject:* RE: Jul SIP monthly meeting



Thanks.



Slide 5

   - Seller PC2 here includes fee that we pay to platform?

Do we get any
   discount to the fee?
- Why is CN SIP so negative on PC2?
What was it previously?
- Can we also show the MP PC2 and SLS P&L as separate row items?

Slide 6

   - Local SIP PC2 is negative at negative?
- Can we also show the MP PC2 and SLS P&L as separate row items?
- CN SIP is growing very slowly MOM?

At such a low base, I would have
   thought that it should be growing much faster?
- WH Cross selling does not feel like it is growing much as well?

Have
   we open it up to Local/CB FBS yet?

Slide 7

   - Any sense why IDMY is 1 more day longer than IDSG?

Slide 8

   - Where did we land on the fraud situation?

Slide 9

   - Do not quite understand what the issue is here. Think the our main
   contribution for these markets will come from CNSIP, so we can rely on CN
   SLS side?

Slide 12

   - Growth feels very low for the # of new SKUs that we brought live?

Do
   we know why?
Are the numbers within expectation?

Slide 14

   - For new sellers, why % of SKUs dropping?
- For rejected sellers, acceptance rate is just too low?

And why % of
   SKU acceptance also so low?
- What are the reasons for rejection right now?

We have to address it 1
   by 1?

Slide 15

   - #1/#3/#4 are probably all not valid?

How exactly are we addressing
   this?

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

Slide 5

   -

   Seller PC2 here includes fee that we pay to platform? Do we get any
   discount to the fee?
   -

      Yes, seller PC2 includes platform fee (commission, service fee,
      transaction fee, infrastructure fee) as SIP cost (markup in SIP listing
      price)
      -

      We do not get any discount to the fee, we pay the same as a normal MP
      seller
      -

   Why is CN SIP so negative on PC2? What was it previously?


CNSIP:


   -

   Seller PC2
   -

      Jun: Seller PC2 became negative due to reinvesting 5% GMV to counter
      revenue from mgt fee removal (~$160k) and OPEX deterioration (~$40k) and
      incubate new SKUs
      -

      May: slightly positive as our target is breakeven
      -

   MP PC2
   -

      Continue to be worsened MoM from May to Jul due to higher platform
      PRM cost (generic to all sellers, not dedicated PRM to CNSIP)



   -

   Can we also show the MP PC2 and SLS P&L as separate row items?


Local SIP:


   -

   Seller PC2
   -

      Jul is on track to target PC2 (-3% for MYSG while breakeven for other
      lanes)
      -

      Jun and May are higher than target due to under-spending of the
      budget because actual budget is higher than projected
      -

         Jun: one-time passback from ID on fraud case claw back (~$400k)
         -

         May: higher than expected passback from export markets from
         regional BPSF reduction initiative
         -

   MP PC2
   -

      Jul proj vs Jun: maintain at similar level as Jun
      -

      Jun vs May: MoM worsened due to higher platform PRM cost (generic to
      all sellers, not dedicated to local SIP)
      -

   SLS PC2
   -

      Jul proj vs Jun: MoM worsened due to transitioning to new vendors in
      Jul and will be adjusting ASF on 22 Jul for 14 lanes
      -

      Jun vs May: MoM improved due to ADO growth


Slide 6

   -

Hi Ian,

Slide 5

   -

   Seller PC2 here includes fee that we pay to platform? Do we get any
   discount to the fee?
   -

      Yes, seller PC2 includes platform fee (commission, service fee,
      transaction fee, infrastructure fee) as SIP cost (markup in SIP listing
      price)
      -

      We do not get any discount to the fee, we pay the same as a normal MP
      seller
      -

   Why is CN SIP so negative on PC2? What was it previously?

   -

      Yes, seller PC2 includes platform fee (commission, service fee,
      transaction fee, infrastructure fee) as SIP cost (markup in SIP listing
      price)
      -

      We do not get any discount to the fee, we pay the same as a normal MP
      seller
      -

   Why is CN SIP so negative on PC2? What was it previously?

Hi Ian,

Noted. We will assess overall ROI, taking into account topline growth
versus incremental staff cost, and then back-estimate the right level of
trade-off between deflection rate and CSAT.

Hi Ian,

Please find the updates on the follow-up actions in the attachment. In
summary,

1. SIP UE

   - Local SIP UE remains healthy at $0.69 for Q2-25 despite fraud loss in
   Apr
   - CNSIP UE $0.56 in Jun, half of Apr/May due to PC2 reinvestment to
   counter management fee removal, but still higher than CNCB ($0.46)
   - Key reasons for higher CNSIP UE vs CNCB MP are 1) higher MP PC2 due to
   higher ads takerate; 2) higher SLS PnL due to higher mix of parcels to
   Latam lanes with higher PPO (same SLS rate card)

2. CNSIP ramp up

   - Propose to migrate new SKUs acquired earliest possible to generate
   organic topline and marketing budget to fund more SKU incubation; no CB
   ops/listing BPM concerns
   - Incubate new SKUs the same approach as local SIP, use budget from 1)
   new seller commission waiver; 2) new SKU organic orders; 3) import market
   seller programs
   - Project 28.3k ADO from new SKUs by Dec


3. Chatbot rollout (DR/CSAT trade-off)

   - Higher Chatbot CSAT from new chatbot leads to higher shop CSAT and
   higher order conversion, however staff cost of incremental orders converted
   is high at $7-$27 per order
   - However overall impact to SIP EBITDA is manageable as total staff cost
   is small compared to the total SIP profit
   - Propose to target 52% chatbot CSAT + 56% chatbot Deflection, to reach
   66% shop CSAT (+11pp vs now), with minimal negative impact to overall SIP
   economics
